text: Your option will vest over four years, with 25% vesting after one year of service and the remainder vesting monthly thereafter.
label: standard
why: Four-year vesting with a one-year cliff is common for startup employees.
market_standard: Four-year vesting with a one-year cliff and monthly vesting after the cliff.
counter: No change needed unless negotiating for acceleration or credit for prior service.

text: No vesting acceleration will apply upon a change in control, termination, or any other event.
label: unusual
why: No acceleration can leave the employee exposed if the company is sold or the role is eliminated.
market_standard: Senior hires often ask for single-trigger or double-trigger acceleration, especially after an acquisition.
counter: Add double-trigger acceleration for 50% to 100% of unvested equity if employment is terminated without cause or for good reason within 12 months after a change in control.
demo_hook: No protection if the company sells.

text: Vested options must be exercised within 30 days after termination of employment.
label: red-flag
why: A short exercise window can force the employee to spend cash quickly or lose vested equity.
market_standard: Many candidates negotiate an extended post-termination exercise window.
counter: Extend the post-termination exercise window for vested options to at least 12 months, and preferably the full option term where permitted.
demo_hook: You can lose vested equity after 30 days.

text: Employee assigns to company all inventions, discoveries, works, ideas, code, designs, and intellectual property created before, during, or after employment that relate in any way to company business.
label: red-flag
why: This can capture pre-existing side projects and future work outside the job.
market_standard: IP assignment should be limited to work created during employment using company resources or related to assigned duties.
counter: Limit assignment to IP created during employment that relates to company business or uses company resources, and list excluded prior inventions in an exhibit.
demo_hook: Your side project may become theirs.

text: For 12 months after employment, employee may not work for, advise, invest in, or assist any business that competes with company.
label: red-flag
why: Broad non-competes can limit future employment and may be unenforceable or restricted in some states.
market_standard: Restrictive covenants should be narrow, lawful, and focused on confidential information and non-solicitation where permitted.
counter: Delete the non-compete. Replace with a confidentiality covenant and any lawful, narrowly tailored non-solicitation obligations.
demo_hook: This can block your next job.

text: Employment is at will and may be terminated by either you or the company at any time, with or without cause or notice.
label: standard
why: At-will employment is standard in many US startup offer letters.
market_standard: At-will language is common, though executives may negotiate severance or notice.
counter: No change needed for most roles; senior candidates may ask for severance if terminated without cause.

text: You are not entitled to severance, continued benefits, acceleration, or other payments upon termination.
label: unusual
why: For senior or hard-to-replace roles, no severance may be worth negotiating.
market_standard: Senior hires often ask for 1-3 months of severance and benefits continuation for termination without cause.
counter: Add severance equal to 8 weeks of base salary and benefits continuation if terminated without cause, subject to a standard release.

text: Any bonus is fully discretionary and may be modified, reduced, or eliminated at any time.
label: unusual
why: A discretionary-only bonus may not be meaningful if it was part of the compensation pitch.
market_standard: Target bonuses should identify target amount, criteria, and eligibility timing.
counter: State the target bonus amount, eligibility period, payment timing, and any objective performance criteria.

text: If you leave or are terminated for any reason within 24 months, you must repay all relocation expenses and signing bonus amounts.
label: red-flag
why: Repayment after involuntary termination is harsh and 24 months can be long.
market_standard: Repayment obligations are usually prorated and often excluded for termination without cause.
counter: Repayment should be prorated monthly and waived if employment ends due to termination without cause, layoff, disability, death, or company breach.
demo_hook: You owe money if they lay you off.

text: Employee must keep confidential all information learned during employment, whether or not public, known, or independently developed.
label: red-flag
why: Confidentiality should not cover public information or knowledge independently developed outside the company.
market_standard: Confidentiality obligations include standard carveouts for public information, prior knowledge, and independent development.
counter: Add carveouts for information that is public, already known without duty of confidentiality, independently developed, or lawfully received from another source.

text: Employee may not engage in outside employment, consulting, advisory work, open-source work, or business activity without company approval.
label: unusual
why: This can block harmless side projects, advising, or open-source contributions.
market_standard: Outside activity limits should focus on conflicts of interest, use of company resources, and interference with job duties.
counter: Permit outside activities that do not compete with company, use company confidential information or resources, or interfere with job duties.

text: The equity grant is subject to approval by the board of directors and the terms of the company's equity plan.
label: standard
why: Board approval is common, but candidates should ask for the grant size and plan documents.
market_standard: Offer letters should state the number of shares or options, exercise price methodology, vesting, and approval process.
counter: Confirm the number of shares/options, fully diluted ownership percentage, vesting schedule, exercise price methodology, and plan documents.

text: You will receive an option to purchase 50,000 shares of common stock.
label: unusual
why: Share count alone does not tell the candidate ownership percentage or dilution context.
market_standard: Candidates should understand the fully diluted percentage and latest valuation context.
counter: Provide the approximate fully diluted ownership percentage represented by the grant and current total shares outstanding.

text: Employee agrees to binding arbitration for all employment-related claims and waives class, collective, and representative actions.
label: unusual
why: Arbitration and class waivers can limit procedural rights and shared claims.
market_standard: Dispute terms should comply with applicable law and preserve non-waivable rights.
counter: Confirm the clause preserves all non-waivable rights and uses a neutral arbitration provider with company-paid fees where required.

text: This offer is contingent on satisfactory completion of background checks, employment eligibility verification, and execution of company agreements.
label: standard
why: Contingencies are common, but candidates should review all required agreements before resigning from a current job.
market_standard: Contingencies should be clear and all required agreements should be provided before acceptance.
counter: Ask for all required agreements and policies before accepting and before resigning from any current role.
